title: Shattering Sour Canadian Morbier Bake
description: A post-workout, fermented dish featuring Morbier cheese, perfect for a Canadian twist on a traditional recipe. To serve, gently shatter the crispy banana leaf vessel.
instructions: Prepare the banana leaf vessel by greasing it with coconut oil and seasoning with salt, black pepper, and a pinch of fleur de sel.
In a large mixing bowl, combine the Morbier cheese, whole wheat flour, mashed banana, baking soda, honey (if using), and ferment-jang (if using).
Mix the ingredients until you get a smooth, even dough.
Transfer the dough to the prepared banana leaf vessel, spreading it evenly to the edges.
Cover the vessel with plastic wrap and let it ferment at room temperature for 2-3 hours, or until it's slightly puffed and has a tangy aroma.
Place the vessel in the preheated oven and bake for 20-25 minutes, or until the edges are golden brown and the center is set.
Remove the vessel from the oven and let it cool for a few minutes.
Gently shatter the banana leaf by placing a spatula underneath and carefully twisting it.
Serve the Shattering Sour Canadian Morbier Bake warm, garnished with fresh thyme and parsley.
Store any leftovers in an airtight container in the refrigerator for up to 3 days.
Reheat the leftovers in the oven or microwave before serving.
Note: If you don't have ferment-jang, you can omit it or substitute it with another type of fermentation starter culture.
Note: To enhance the fermentation flavor, you can add a few drops of apple cider vinegar or lemon juice to the dough.
Note: If you prefer a crisper crust, you can bake the Morbier Bake for an additional 5-10 minutes.
